channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: CWG Live updates: Beautiful with a bright moon tonight. More nice days to come. - The Washington Post
item.description:
1. CWG Live updates: Beautiful with a bright moon tonight. More nice days to come.  The Washington Post

  3. How and when to watch May’s blue moon  CNN
  4. What to know about a rare blue micromoon rising this weekend  PBS
  5. How to Best See This Weekend’s Rare Blue Micromoon  Time Magazine

item.description:

item.description:

  2. NASA’s Hubble captures gorgeous new photo of a spiral galaxy as it wanders through the Virgo Cluster  Scientific American

  5. NASA's Hubble Telescope Captures Active Galaxy M88 With Giant Black Hole  NDTV

item.title: Pigeons navigate using magnetic sensors in livers — study - DW.com
item.description:
1. Pigeons navigate using magnetic sensors in livers — study  DW.com
  2. How do pigeons sense Earth's magnetic field? Scientists say they’ve solved the mystery.  National Geographic
  3. Pigeons use their livers to sense Earth’s magnetic field  Popular Science
  4. Pigeons may be navigating with their liver, study suggests  AP News
  5. To travel far on cloudy days, pigeons listen to their gut  CNN

item.title: NASA's next major space telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman, is expected to find roughly 100,000 new transiting planets in just five years — along with the largest catalogue ever assembled of rogue worlds drifting through the galaxy without a star to orbit - Space Daily
item.description:
1. NASA's next major space telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman, is expected to find roughly 100,000 new transiting planets in just five years — along with the largest catalogue ever assembled of rogue worlds drifting through the galaxy without a star to orbit  Space Daily
  2. NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look  NASA (.gov)
  3. NASA's Nancy Grace Roman telescope aims to unlock dark energy secrets  KOMO
  4. Peering into the Milky Way's far side, Roman could unveil 100,000 worlds  Phys.org
  5. Roman telescope will observe an unexplored part of the Milky Way  Universe Space Tech

item.title: Welcome to the Gas Giant Planet Where Clouds Turn to Stone - Good News Network
item.description:
1. Welcome to the Gas Giant Planet Where Clouds Turn to Stone  Good News Network

  4. NASA’s Webb telescope discovers a planet where rock clouds vanish every night  ScienceDaily

item.title: Webb reveals black hole that formed before its galaxy - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Webb reveals black hole that formed before its galaxy  Phys.org

  3. Little Red Dot Abell2744-QSO1 (NIRCam Image)  NASA Science (.gov)
  4. The Weirdness of Early Universe SMBHs Gets Even Weirder  Universe Today

item.title: Reported ancient mammoth bones found deep in Alaska were actually whales from 250 miles away - Yahoo
item.description: Reported ancient mammoth bones found deep in Alaska were actually whales from 250 miles away  YahooSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: There is a region of space called the Boötes void, around 330 million light years wide and almost completely empty, and if our galaxy sat at its centre, we might not have discovered that other galaxies existed until well into the twentieth century. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. There is a region of space called the Boötes void, around 330 million light years wide and almost completely empty, and if our galaxy sat at its centre, we might not have discovered that other galaxies existed until well into the twentieth century.  Space Daily
  2. The Emptiest Places in the Universe Might Contain Its Best Secrets  WIRED

item.title: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to roughly five times the temperature of the surface of the Sun, and it is the violent expansion of that superheated air that creates the crack of thunder. - Space Daily
item.description: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to roughly five times the temperature of the surface of the Sun, and it is the violent expansion of that superheated air that creates the crack of thunder.  Space Daily

item.title: Here's how Tyrannosaurus ended up with a big head and small arms - Reuters
item.description:
1. Here's how Tyrannosaurus ended up with a big head and small arms  Reuters
  2. Tyrannosaurus Rex and Other Terrifying Predatory Dinosaurs Had Itty-Bitty Arms. Scientists May Have Finally Figured Out Why  Smithsonian Magazine
  3. Did the Tiny Arms of Certain Theropods like T. rex Evolve Five Separate Times?  Answers in Genesis
  4. T. rex had massive jaws but tiny arms: Scientists now know why  The Independent
  5. Why did T. rex have tiny arms? A new study may finally have the answer  CNN

item.title: Scientists Intrigued by Chunk of Flesh That Refuses to Die After Several Years - Futurism
item.description:
1. Scientists Intrigued by Chunk of Flesh That Refuses to Die After Several Years  Futurism
  2. A severed piece of sea cucumber refused to die, and what happened next could transform medicine  Phys.org
  3. Amputated sea cucumber parts show signs of ‘immortality,’ researchers say  CNN
  4. Severed sea cucumber appendages don’t seem to die  Ars Technica
  5. Sea cucumber tissue heals itself and survives for years after being detached from the body  Earth.com

item.title: 'I would never have guessed it': Unexpected effect is squeezing Mars' atmosphere like toothpaste, experts say - Yahoo
item.description:
1. 'I would never have guessed it': Unexpected effect is squeezing Mars' atmosphere like toothpaste, experts say  Yahoo
  2. MAVEN Spacecraft Finds New Plasma Squeezing at Mars  Universe Today
  3. WVU researcher finds a surprising phenomenon in NASA data from Mars  Times West Virginian
  4. 'Very interesting wiggles' in data from silent NASA Mars spacecraft lead to unexpected solar wind discovery  Space
  5. WVU scientist discovers new Mars atmospheric phenomenon using NASA spacecraft data  WV News

item.title: There are more atoms in a single glass of water than there are glasses of water in all the world's oceans combined — and if you marked every atom in one glass, dumped it into the sea, and waited for the oceans to mix completely, every glass of water on Earth - Space Daily
item.description: There are more atoms in a single glass of water than there are glasses of water in all the world's oceans combined — and if you marked every atom in one glass, dumped it into the sea, and waited for the oceans to mix completely, every glass of water on Earth  Space Daily

item.title: A day on Earth is described as 24 hours, but the planet has been slowing down for billions of years — and growth rings in ancient coral fossils show that 380 million years ago, hundreds of millions of years before any dinosaur existed, an Earth day was only ab - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A day on Earth is described as 24 hours, but the planet has been slowing down for billions of years — and growth rings in ancient coral fossils show that 380 million years ago, hundreds of millions of years before any dinosaur existed, an Earth day was only ab  Space Daily
  2. Something ‘unprecedented’ is now happening to Earth’s rotation, scientists say  BBC Science Focus Magazine
  3. A Day on Earth Is Getting Longer as The Planet's Rotation Slows  Magzter
  4. Goodbye to the 24-hour day: from this date onwards, days on Earth will last 25 hours  ECOticias.com
  5. A Day on Earth Is Getting Longer as the Planet’s Rotation Slows  WSJ

item.title: The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have struck a planet that was already in serious trouble — a new Johns Hopkins study has found evidence of a separate ecological crisis that began about 30,000 years before the impact, coincident with a high-vo - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have struck a planet that was already in serious trouble — a new Johns Hopkins study has found evidence of a separate ecological crisis that began about 30,000 years before the impact, coincident with a high-vo  Space Daily
  2. Fungi Bloomed Twice around End-Cretaceous Mass Extinction  Sci.News
  3. When The Dinosaur-Killing Asteroid Hit, This Life-Form Feasted On The Death  ScienceAlert
  4. Story of dinosaur-killer meteorite impact told in vivid detail  The Guardian
  5. Extinction: What were conditions like on Earth when the dinosaur-killing asteroid crashed into the planet?  FOX Weather

item.title: The oxygen in Earth's atmosphere is essential to almost every animal alive today — but when it first started accumulating in the air roughly 2.4 billion years ago, it triggered the most lethal pollution event in the planet's history, wiping out the vast majority of spec - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The oxygen in Earth's atmosphere is essential to almost every animal alive today — but when it first started accumulating in the air roughly 2.4 billion years ago, it triggered the most lethal pollution event in the planet's history, wiping out the vast majority of spec  Space Daily
  2. Asteroid crater discovery in South Korea may reveal how Earth first filled with oxygen  Yahoo
  3. Earth's oxygen-rich atmosphere may owe its existence to cold subduction  Phys.org
  4. Want an oxygen-rich atmosphere? Stuff oxygen’s friends in the mantle.  Ars Technica
  5. Asteroid Impact Craters May Have Helped Create Early Habitats for Oxygen-Producing Life  The Debrief

item.title: The Sun might look yellow, but seen from space without an atmosphere filtering its light, the sun is actually white — and the yellow color we see from Earth is the result of our atmosphere scattering blue wavelengths away, in the same physics that makes the sk - Space Daily
item.description: The Sun might look yellow, but seen from space without an atmosphere filtering its light, the sun is actually white — and the yellow color we see from Earth is the result of our atmosphere scattering blue wavelengths away, in the same physics that makes the sk  Space Daily

item.description: Scientists Challenge a 70-Year-Old Theory of Language With a Surprising Discovery  SciTechDaily

item.title: Tardigrades reveal extreme heat-blocking survival trick while in tun state - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Tardigrades reveal extreme heat-blocking survival trick while in tun state  Phys.org
  2. Tardigrades can survive being boiled, frozen to near absolute zero, blasted with radiation, and exposed to the vacuum of space, and they do it by drying themselves into a glass-like state where every cellular process stops and they wait, sometimes for decades  Space Daily

item.title: An Unknown Object Passed Between Earth And A Star In The Large Magellanic Cloud, In An Event That Lasted An Hour - IFLScience
item.description:
1. An Unknown Object Passed Between Earth And A Star In The Large Magellanic Cloud, In An Event That Lasted An Hour  IFLScience
  2. Something Just Passed Between Us and a Distant Star.  Universe Today
  3. A glow lasting only 1 hour observed in space in 2019 may have revealed a primordial black hole with a mass of 3 moons, associated with dark matter and considered 100,000 times more likely than common stellar objects by scientists in the study.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  4. A Flash in the Sky Reveals a Possible Object That Passed for an Hour Between Our Galaxy and the Large Magellanic Cloud  La Brújula Verde
  5. A mysterious object passed in front of a distant star for one hour, and it may be a primordial black hole  MSN

item.title: The surface of the Sun is around 5,500 degrees, but its outer atmosphere is more than a million degrees hotter, and after decades of work physicists still cannot fully explain how the cooler layer heats the one above it. - Space Daily
item.description: The surface of the Sun is around 5,500 degrees, but its outer atmosphere is more than a million degrees hotter, and after decades of work physicists still cannot fully explain how the cooler layer heats the one above it.  Space Daily

item.title: Astronomers have found a cloud of water vapour around a distant quasar holding something like 140 trillion times all the water in Earth's oceans, the largest reservoir of water ever discovered anywhere in the universe. - Space Daily
item.description: Astronomers have found a cloud of water vapour around a distant quasar holding something like 140 trillion times all the water in Earth's oceans, the largest reservoir of water ever discovered anywhere in the universe.  Space Daily
